name: Milton Berle
abstract: Milton Berle (born Mendel Berlinger; Yiddish: ‏מענדעל בערלינגער; July 12, 1908 – March 27, 2002) was an American actor and comedian. His career as an entertainer spanned over eight decades, first in silent films and on stage as a child actor, then in radio, movies and television.

concerts.label: Benefit: U.S. Convalescent Hospital at Camp Upton, L.I.

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Greer
about_quotes.evidence: Milton Berle, Uncle Miltie, he was a big thing on television, so we are sitting up there... He used to always come running out at that time... and I say, 'Hey man, you better get all the laughs you can, because we're coming through.'

about_quotes.speaker: Buster Williams
about_quotes.evidence: Milton Berle, Bing Crosby, you know, and we were talking one day during the break and we were talking about all the attention that he was getting now.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buster Williams interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: they got everybody else's name up there like Lou Holtz and Milton Berle -- many of the acts that played there but they didn't name Buck and Bubbles.

about_quotes.speaker: Spiegle Willcox and Al Gallodoro
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Bob Hope back in 1930, ‘31, ‘32, before he was even known, Milton Berle, the ventriloquist, Edgar Bergen, the original Three Stooges.
about_quotes.doc_title: Spiegle Willcox and Al Gallodoro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Norwich, New York, November 14, 1995
